title: astragalus dianthus
intro: Astragalus dianthus, also known as milk vetch or dune milk vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the coastal dunes of the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to make various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting respiratory health. Additionally, Astragalus dianthus has been used in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance energy and vitality, making it a popular herb among practitioners of traditional Chinese medicine.
